hero.title1: Menopause should not steal your sleep,
hero.desc: Hot flushes, night sweats, mood swings, and vaginal dryness can make you feel like you're not in control of your own body. At Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur, we help you understand your symptoms, your options, and what "safe, personalised HRT" actually looks like.
hero.address: LG 10, Lower Ground Floor, Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang, 50450 Kuala Lumpur
hero.trust1: Central Kuala Lumpur location for discreet visits
hero.trust2: Step by step decision making based on your symptoms, health history, and comfort level
hero.trust3: Evidence-based care: major menopause guidance consistently states hormone therapy is the most effective treatment

understanding.title: What is menopause and perimenopause
understanding.p1: Menopause is officially diagnosed after 12 months without a period, and many women go through a transition phase called perimenopause before that. During perimenopause, hormones can swing. That is why symptoms can feel random.
understanding.p2: Hospitals in Malaysia commonly explain that menopause often happens between about 45 and 55, but it can occur earlier or later.

hrtMht.title: HRT vs MHT
hrtMht.explanation: They usually mean the same idea: replacing hormones (mainly oestrogen, and sometimes progestogen) to reduce menopause symptoms and protect long term health when appropriate. Pantai KL describes Menopause Hormone Therapy as oestrogen therapy in different forms, and notes women with an intact uterus need additional progestogen therapy.

symptoms.title: Symptoms that make women search
symptoms.subtitle: "menopause HRT Kuala Lumpur"
symptoms.note: If you are reading this and thinking "that's me," you're not alone.
symptoms.s1Desc: This is the number one reason women start HRT. The North American Menopause Society notes hormone therapy remains the most effective treatment.
symptoms.s2Desc: Waking up exhausted, mood changes, anxiety, irritability
symptoms.s3Desc: Pain during intimacy, urinary symptoms like urgency or leakage. Many women suffer quietly here.
symptoms.s4Desc: Menopause hormone therapy can help prevent bone loss and reduce fracture risk in appropriate women.
symptoms.pantaiNote: Pantai's HRT page lists these exact types of symptoms as reasons women consider HRT.

types.title: Types of Menopause HRT in Malaysia
types.oestrogenDesc: Usually used if you have had a hysterectomy (uterus removed). Gleneagles notes oestrogen-only HRT is typically recommended for women who have had their uterus removed.
types.combinedDesc: Used if you still have a uterus. Pantai KL explains women with an intact uterus need additional progestogen therapy.
types.methodsTitle: Ways to take HRT (and why this matters)
types.methodsNote: Pantai KL lists multiple oestrogen forms such as tablets, gels, creams, or sprays.
types.safetyDesc: The Menopause Society Australia summary of NAMS 2022 highlights that transdermal routes and lower doses may decrease risk of venous thromboembolism and stroke. In plain words: for many women, a patch or gel may be a safer fit than a tablet, depending on health history.

consultation.title: What happens at a Menopause HRT consultation
consultation.subtitle: A good clinic does not start with a prescription. It starts with clarity.
consultation.s1Desc: We talk about what you feel, when it started, what triggers it, and what you have tried.
consultation.s2Desc: Your history matters: breast cancer family history, clot risk, blood pressure, migraines, smoking, and your uterus status.
consultation.s3Desc: Non-hormonal first steps, local vaginal therapy, systemic HRT, route choice based on risk profile.
consultation.s4Desc: HRT should be reviewed regularly. Personalisation and periodic re-evaluation are core themes.

candidate.goodTitle: Who is a good candidate for Menopause HRT?
candidate.goodDesc: Guidance consistently supports a favourable benefit-risk ratio for healthy symptomatic women under 60, or within about 10 years of menopause onset, assuming no contraindications.
candidate.g1: Symptoms are affecting daily life (sleep, work, relationships)
candidate.g2: You understand the benefits and risks
candidate.g3: You are ready for follow-up reviews and monitoring
candidate.avoidDesc: NHS guidance notes HRT can slightly increase breast cancer risk and is usually avoided in women who have had breast cancer. Pantai's cancer risk article also notes HRT may not be recommended for women with a history of breast cancer, blood clots, or certain cardiovascular conditions.
candidate.c2: Stroke or severe cardiovascular disease history

risks.title: Side effects and risks, explained without fear
risks.subtitle: Most women tolerate HRT well, but you deserve honest expectations.
risks.r1Desc: Breast tenderness, mild bloating, headaches, spotting or bleeding in early months
risks.r2Desc: NHS explains combined HRT can slightly increase breast cancer risk, with risk rising with longer duration and falling after stopping. Breast Cancer Now also notes vaginal HRT with low-dose oestrogen does not increase breast cancer risk.
risks.r3Desc: Risk depends on your baseline risk factors and the HRT route. Transdermal routes may reduce VTE and stroke risk compared with oral, according to NAMS 2022 summaries.
risks.fdaTitle: A very recent update you might have seen online
risks.fdaDesc: In November 2025, the FDA announced it removed the long-standing boxed warning from many hormone-based menopause medicines in the US, arguing newer evidence supports more personalised risk discussions. This change is still debated by experts. This does not automatically change Malaysian practice, but it shows the global conversation has moved toward "right patient, right timing, right route" rather than fear-based avoidance.

comparison.title: HRT vs non-hormonal treatments
comparison.hrtDesc: Most effective treatment for vasomotor symptoms and GSM
comparison.nonHrtDesc: The Asia-Pacific Menopause Federation consensus notes non-hormonal options like SSRIs, SNRIs, clonidine, and gabapentin can help symptoms, but they are generally less effective than MHT.
comparison.nh3: Strength training for bone and mood

cost.title: Cost of Menopause HRT in Malaysia
cost.subtitle: There is no single "HRT price" because the cost depends on:
cost.c3: Type of HRT (patch vs pill vs gel)
cost.tipDesc: ask for an itemised plan (consult + tests + monthly medication + follow-up review). That is the fairest way to compare clinics.

competitor.comp1Desc: Strong educational content. Clear description of MHT, need for progestogen if uterus intact, and bone benefits.

faq.a1: For many healthy women under 60 or within about 10 years of menopause onset, the benefit-risk profile is favourable when there are no contraindications and therapy is reviewed regularly.
faq.q2: What symptoms does HRT help the most?
faq.a2: HRT is the most effective treatment for hot flushes and night sweats, and it is also highly effective for vaginal and urinary menopause symptoms (GSM).
faq.q3: Do I need progesterone with oestrogen?
faq.a3: If you still have a uterus, yes, you usually need a progestogen to protect the womb lining. Pantai KL states women with an intact uterus need additional progestogen therapy.
faq.q4: What is the safest type of HRT?
faq.a4: There is no single safest option for everyone. Route and dose matter. Summaries of NAMS 2022 state transdermal routes and lower doses may reduce clot and stroke risk.
faq.a5: Weight change often happens around menopause due to hormonal shifts and ageing. Many women feel better on HRT because sleep improves and activity becomes easier, but HRT is not a weight loss drug.
faq.q6: How quickly does HRT start working?
faq.a6: Some women feel improvements within days to a couple of weeks, especially for hot flushes and sleep, though it varies. Pantai's HRT cancer risk FAQ notes symptom improvement can be seen within several days to a couple of weeks for some women.
faq.q7: Does HRT increase breast cancer risk?
faq.a7: Combined HRT slightly increases breast cancer risk, and risk increases with longer use. Risk generally falls again after stopping.
faq.q8: Can I use vaginal oestrogen if I cannot take systemic HRT?
faq.a8: Many guidance sources note low-dose vaginal oestrogen is treated differently from systemic HRT, and Breast Cancer Now states vaginal HRT does not increase breast cancer risk in the general population. Always confirm with your doctor for your personal history.
faq.q9: What if I cannot take HRT?
faq.a9: Non-hormonal options such as SSRIs, SNRIs, clonidine, and gabapentin may help, though they are generally less effective than MHT.
faq.q10: How long can I stay on HRT?
faq.a10: Duration is personalised. Major position statements emphasise shared decision making and periodic review to reassess benefits and risks.
faq.q11: Do I need blood tests to start HRT?
faq.a11: Not always for menopause diagnosis, but clinicians may order tests based on symptoms and risk factors. Many providers in Malaysia combine clinical evaluation with appropriate screening.
faq.q12: Where can I get menopause HRT in Kuala Lumpur?
faq.a12: Menopause care is offered by hospitals and clinics in Malaysia. If you want a central KL option, Nexus Clinic Kuala Lumpur is listed at Wisma UOA II, Jalan Pinang, with a published contact number.
